title: Mrs Mai, you are not old
summary: A girl asks her seventy-year-old neighbour, a tailor, why she still works. The tailor gives a short answer — and offers the girl a sewing lesson.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Mrs Mai, you are old. Why do you still work?

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: I am seventy. I am not old.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: My grandmother is seventy. She does not work.

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: Every person is different, Linh. I like my work.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: What do you like?

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: I like the cloth. I like the colours. I like to make a shirt that fits. People come back to me. That is a good feeling.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Are you tired?

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: Yes, sometimes. But I am happy.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: When will you stop?

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: I will stop when my hands cannot work.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: And then what?

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: Then I will sit at this window. I will watch the new tailor work. I will say, 'That line is too long.' She will smile and fix it.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Will she be your daughter?

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: No, she will be a girl I taught. Maybe she will be you.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Me? I do not know how to sew.

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: I will teach you. Come on Saturday. Bring a small piece of cloth and a needle. The first lesson is free.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: And the second lesson?

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: The second is free too. The third costs one sentence.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: What sentence?

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: 'Mrs Mai, you are not old.'

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: I can say that now!

body.speaker: Mrs Mai
body.text: Then I am paid. Come on Saturday.

annotations.meaningVi: mỗi người mỗi khác (thành ngữ chỉ sự khác biệt cá nhân)
đến vào thứ Bảy (lời mời lịch sự)
tốn, có giá (chi phí, không chỉ tiền mà cả thứ khác)
tôi được trả công rồi (thể bị động; ở đây nghĩa bóng)
Đường chỉ đó dài quá ('line' trong may: đường may, đường chỉ)

grammarNotes.title: Future with 'will'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will + V' để nói về tương lai. Trong bài: 'I will stop when my hands cannot work.' (Tôi sẽ nghỉ khi tay tôi không thể làm việc nữa.) / 'I will teach you.' / 'She will be a girl I taught.' Công thức: chủ ngữ + will + động từ nguyên thể.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will + verb' to talk about the future. From the dialogue: 'I will stop when my hands cannot work.' / 'I will teach you.' / 'She will be a girl I taught.' Form: subject + will + base verb.

cloze.template:
Linh: Mrs Mai, you are old. Why do you still work?
Mrs Mai: I am {{b1}}. I am not old.
Linh: My {{b2}} is seventy. She does not work.
Mrs Mai: Every person is different, Linh. I like my work.
Linh: What do you like?
Mrs Mai: I like the {{b3}}. I like the colours. I like to make a {{b4}} that fits. People come back to me. That is a good feeling.
Linh: Are you tired?
Mrs Mai: Yes, sometimes. But I am {{b5}}.
Linh: When will you {{b6}}?
Mrs Mai: I will stop when my {{b7}} cannot work.
Linh: And then what?
Mrs Mai: Then I will sit at this {{b8}}. I will watch the new tailor work. I will say, 'That line is too long.' She will smile and fix it.
Linh: Will she be your {{b9}}?
Mrs Mai: No, she will be a girl I taught. Maybe she will be you.
Linh: Me? I do not know how to sew.
Mrs Mai: I will teach you. Come on {{b10}}. Bring a small piece of cloth and a {{b11}}. The first lesson is {{b12}}.
Linh: And the second lesson?
Mrs Mai: The second is free too. The third costs one sentence.
Linh: What sentence?
Mrs Mai: 'Mrs Mai, you are not old.'
Linh: I can say that now!
Mrs Mai: Then I am paid. Come on Saturday.

blanks.explanation: She likes to make a shirt that fits.
She will stop when her hands cannot work — the tools of her job.
She will sit at the window to watch.
Linh asks if the new tailor will be Mrs Mai's daughter.
She will need cloth and a needle to sew.

translationVi:
Linh: Bà Mai ơi, bà già rồi mà. Sao bà vẫn làm việc?
Bà Mai: Bà bảy mươi. Bà không già đâu.
Linh: Bà nội cháu cũng bảy mươi. Bà ấy không làm việc.
Bà Mai: Mỗi người mỗi khác, Linh. Bà thích công việc của mình.
Linh: Bà thích gì ạ?
Bà Mai: Bà thích vải. Bà thích các màu. Bà thích may một cái áo vừa người. Khách quay lại tìm bà. Cảm giác đó tốt.
Linh: Bà có mệt không ạ?
Bà Mai: Có, đôi khi. Nhưng bà vui.
Linh: Khi nào bà sẽ nghỉ ạ?
Bà Mai: Bà sẽ nghỉ khi đôi tay bà không làm việc được nữa.
Linh: Rồi sau đó thì sao ạ?
Bà Mai: Rồi bà sẽ ngồi ở cái cửa sổ này. Bà sẽ nhìn người thợ may mới làm. Bà sẽ nói: 'Đường đó dài quá.' Cô ấy sẽ mỉm cười và sửa lại.
Linh: Cô ấy sẽ là con gái bà ạ?
Bà Mai: Không, cô ấy sẽ là một cô gái mà bà đã dạy. Có khi sẽ là cháu.
Linh: Cháu á? Cháu đâu biết khâu.
Bà Mai: Bà sẽ dạy cháu. Thứ Bảy này đến đi. Mang một mảnh vải nhỏ và một cây kim. Buổi học đầu tiên miễn phí.
Linh: Còn buổi thứ hai ạ?
Bà Mai: Buổi thứ hai cũng miễn phí. Buổi thứ ba thì tốn một câu.
Linh: Câu gì ạ?
Bà Mai: 'Bà Mai ơi, bà không già đâu.'
Linh: Cháu nói được câu đó ngay bây giờ!
Bà Mai: Vậy là bà được trả công rồi. Thứ Bảy này nhé.

questions.prompt: What does Mrs Mai do for work?
options: She makes shirts and works with cloth — she is a tailor
questions.explanation: 'I like the cloth… I like to make a shirt that fits.'
questions.hint: Look at what she likes about her work.

questions.prompt: Why does Mrs Mai still work?
options: She likes the cloth and the colours, and people come back to her
questions.explanation: 'I like the cloth. I like the colours. I like to make a shirt that fits. People come back to me.'
questions.hint: Look at her long reply about what she likes.

options: Yes, sometimes — but she is happy
questions.hint: Look at her reply about being tired.

questions.prompt: When will Mrs Mai stop working?
questions.explanation: 'I will stop when my hands cannot work.'
questions.hint: Look at her answer about stopping.

questions.prompt: What will Mrs Mai do after she stops?
options: Sit at the window and watch the new tailor work
questions.explanation: 'Then I will sit at this window. I will watch the new tailor work.'
questions.hint: Look at her answer about what comes after stopping.

questions.prompt: Who might be the new tailor one day?
options: A girl Mrs Mai taught — maybe even Linh
questions.explanation: 'She will be a girl I taught. Maybe she will be you.'
questions.hint: Look at Mrs Mai's reply to 'Will she be your daughter?'

questions.prompt: What should Linh bring on Saturday?
options: A small piece of cloth and a needle
questions.explanation: 'Bring a small piece of cloth and a needle.'

options: The third lesson — 'Mrs Mai, you are not old.'
questions.hint: Look near the end of the conversation.

questions.prompt: What is the tone of the conversation?
options: Warm and a little teasing — Mrs Mai is happy with her work and gently invites Linh into it
questions.explanation: Mrs Mai turns the conversation into a joke and an invitation; Linh ends by saying she can say the sentence right now.
questions.hint: Notice how they end — laughing and arranging Saturday.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mrs Mai says she will stop when her hands cannot work — and then she will watch a new tailor. Think of someone you know who is older than you. What is something they could teach you so they could watch you do it later?
